title: linaria serpyllifolia subsp. robusta
intro: Linaria serpyllifolia subsp. robusta, also known as self-heal or robust self-heal, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant, a low-growing, herbaceous perennial with blue flowers that belongs to the figwort family. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of the self-heal plant have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Linaria serpyllifolia subsp. robusta include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health benefits. Linaria serpyllifolia subsp. robusta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
